svg.text: Black Swan Tail Risk — When Stops Fail
Stops don't work in flash crashes, gaps, or limit-down moves · sizing must survive 3R+ tail events

g.text: May 6, 2010 · Flash Crash · ES futures -7% in 12 minutes · stops triggered 20+ pts beyond level
August 24, 2015 · circuit breakers · many equity stops filled at -15% gap open
March 16, 2020 · COVID circuit breakers · ES limit-down at -7%, stops queued at next limit
August 5, 2024 · Yen carry unwind · NQ -6% premarket · stops at level filled -2% lower
Lesson: assume 2-3× expected loss in tail events
If 1R sizing makes you uncomfortable surviving 3R single-trade loss, you're over-sized
→ Don't hold large futures positions through FOMC/CPI/NFP overnight without OTM puts
→ Reduce size 50% during known event windows (geopolitical, earnings, central bank surprises)
→ Keep 3-5% of account in long-vol hedges (VIX calls, OTM SPY puts) as portfolio insurance
Rule: max position size = ((max tail loss tolerated as % of equity) ÷ 3) × position size
